QA Gate: All support documentation follows a 3-stage workflow: Draft → Pending QA → Activated. Documents in 'Pending QA' require manager review before activation. Managers can approve & activate or return for revision.

SUPropertyLast ContactDays SinceStageEscalationOwnerSSM Notified
SU-0334Flat 2, Netherthorpe12 Feb16 daysDay 3SSM escalated, further actionD. SharpeYes
SU-0511Unit 5, Burngreave14 Feb14 daysDay 2Call attempted, no answerJ. PetersYes
SU-0289Unit 8, Heeley16 Feb12 daysDay 1Initial call madeM. KhanPending
SU-060324 Burngreave Rise17 Feb11 daysDay 1Initial call madeA. BegumPending
Protocol: Triggered when no contact for 14 days. Day 1: phone call. Day 2: escalate to team leader, attempt visit. Day 3: SSM escalated, compliance team notified, consider welfare check with police. Auto-notifications at each stage. If support staff is off, team leader picks it up via dashboard.

Post-Incident Reviews

Incidents

Log New Incident Gℹ️

Quick Guidance Notes (configurable per organisation)
• Always record the incident as soon as possible after it occurs
• Include all staff and service users involved or present
• Select the correct category — the level will auto-assign based on severity
• If emergency services were called, record the reference number
• Update the risk assessment immediately for Level 2 and Level 3 incidents
• Notify your line manager for all Level 3 incidents within 1 hour
Select a category above
Select a category to see the validation question

PIR-002INC-421 — Deceased SU28 JanA. SheraziPolicy update triggeredComplete
PIR-001INC-398 — Serious assault10 JanT. RichardsonTraining gap identifiedComplete
New procedure created after 2 deaths. AI produces initial case review, but deep human analysis is essential. Lessons learned feed back into training and policy updates.

Welfare Concerns

Open 0
Monitoring 0
Resolved / Closed 0

Log Incident Gℹ️

Quick Guidance Notes (configurable per organisation)
• Always record the incident as soon as possible after it occurs
• Include all staff and service users involved or present
• Select the correct category — the level will auto-assign based on severity
• If emergency services were called, record the reference number
• Update the risk assessment immediately for Level 2 and Level 3 incidents
• Notify your line manager for all Level 3 incidents within 1 hour
Select a category above
Select a category to see the validation question

Log Welfare Concern Gℹ️

Quick Guidance Notes (configurable per organisation)
• Log a welfare concern whenever you observe a change that could affect the service user's wellbeing
• If safeguarding is suspected, tick the safeguarding flag — this triggers additional guidance
• Record exactly what you observed, heard, or were told — use the service user's own words where possible
• Do not investigate safeguarding allegations yourself — refer to your Designated Safeguarding Lead
• All welfare concerns must be reviewed by a manager within 48 hours
• If in doubt, log it — it is better to raise a concern that turns out to be unfounded than to miss one
Safeguarding Concern Guidance: Complete this form if you're concerned that a service user is being harmed or is at risk of harm from: physical abuse, domestic violence/abuse, sexual abuse, psychological/emotional abuse, financial/material abuse, modern slavery, discriminatory abuse, neglect/acts of omission, self-neglect.

Concern Log
Flag a Concern

Welfare Concern — Manager Dashboard

Welfare Concern: Below incident threshold, above normal. Staff can flag when "something doesn't feel right." Manager reviews and decides: resolve, monitor, or escalate to formal incident.
Colour: ORANGE — between green (normal) and amber (incident level 2).

Flag a Concern ℹ️

Use this form when something doesn't feel right — it may not be an incident yet, but you want to flag it for manager review.
Safeguarding Concern Guidance
Complete this form if you are concerned that a service user is being harmed or is at risk of harm including: physical abuse, domestic violence, sexual abuse, psychological/emotional abuse, financial abuse, modern slavery, discriminatory abuse, neglect, or self-neglect.

You must follow your organisation's relevant safeguarding procedures.

This form must be completed immediately or within the timeframe set by your organisation.

Compliance Meeting Report — 26 February 2026

Attendees: A. Sherazi (Chair), T. Richardson, P. Okonkwo, L. Hussain, C. Barnes

1. QA Performance
  • Sheffield at 84% (up 2%)
  • S. Williams and D. Sharpe flagged — coaching sessions scheduled
  • AI refusals now at 0 — our AI processes all care content without restrictions
2. Support Plans
  • 30 expiring in next 14 days — Support staff notified
  • 8-week guide feature working well — 48 generated this month
  • Action: L. Hussain's team has most overdue — priority review needed
3. Incidents
  • 23 open incidents, avg resolution 6.2 days
  • PIR-003 (deceased SU) — 2 of 3 actions complete, safeguarding training update pending
  • INC-446 licence breach at Ecclesall Rd — escalated to high, D. Sharpe investigating
4. Welfare Watch
  • 4 active procedures. SU-0334 at Day 3 — immediate action required
  • Discussed adding SMS notification to Day 1 process
5. Properties
  • 12 properties with zero visits — T. Richardson to coordinate visits by 07 Mar
6. AOB
  • New trainee F. Hassan progressing well (40% through onboarding)
  • Abandonment Policy v3.1 — 89% acknowledged, 3 staff chased
  • Next meeting: 05 March 2026

Theme of the Week

Current Theme
Person-Centred Language in Support Notes
Focus on writing about service users with dignity, using their preferred names, and framing progress positively. Avoid deficit-based language.

Event Log ▼

8-Week Guide Generator — AI-Powered Gap Analysis

AI reviews the last 8 weeks of QA'd support notes against the service user's support plan. It identifies gaps in support delivery and creates a personalised guide for the support staff.
⚠ Disclaimer: This guide identifies areas requiring attention based on patterns found in recent support notes. It is not prescriptive — staff have flexibility in how they address each area.

Top Gaps Identified by AI

Example: SU-0203 — I. Yilmaz

Support Staff: A. Begum | 8 weeks analysed: 03 Jan — 28 Feb 2026 | Notes reviewed: 14
Support Plan Goals vs Delivery
GoalNotes ReferencingCoverageGap
Mental health stability9 of 1464%None
Attend GP appointments3 of 1421%Low coverage
Budgeting & rent5 of 1436%Declining trend
Social engagement1 of 147%Critical gap
Employment readiness0 of 140%Not addressed

AI-Generated Guide for Support Staff

Priority 1: Social engagement (7% coverage)
I. Yilmaz's support plan identifies social isolation as a key risk. Only 1 of 14 notes mentions social activities. Recommendation: discuss local community groups, introduce to peer support network. Target: at least 2 social engagement activities documented in next 4 weeks.
Priority 2: Employment readiness (0% coverage)
No support notes reference employment goals despite it being in the support plan. Recommendation: assess current barriers, discuss job centre referral, explore volunteering as first step. Schedule a dedicated key session for employment planning.
Priority 3: GP appointments (21% coverage)
Coverage declining — last mention was 3 weeks ago. Recommendation: check if recent appointments were attended, establish reminder system, consider accompanying if anxiety is a barrier.
Priority 4: Budgeting & rent (36% coverage, declining)
Recent notes show less focus on financial goals. HB status is currently in payment but needs monitoring. Recommendation: schedule a budgeting review session within 2 weeks.
Guide generated automatically. Support staff and team leader should review and incorporate into next key session. Guide does not replace professional judgement.

Temporary Reassignment (GDPR)

When a support staff member is on leave, their caseload can be temporarily reassigned to another support staff member. Access is GDPR-compliant — the covering staff member only sees the active caseload, not historical data. Access auto-expires when the original staff member returns.

Beacon AI — AI Quality Assurance Check

Select an example note or type your own to see how the AI QA system works.
Support Staff
Service User
Load Example
Support Note (Original)

Beacon AI QA Result

🤖
Processing...
Analysing against Beacon's quality standards
AI Assessment
Scoring Breakdown
Beacon Published Version
Each note is scored by Beacon QA, then polished for spelling, grammar and professionalism. The Beacon Published Version preserves the original meaning — only SPAG and presentation are improved.
Support Staff Verification Required
"Is this an accurate factual depiction of the contact?"

How Beacon AI Works

1
Support staff writes note using structured form (not free text). Sections for contact type, observations, outcomes, next steps.
2
AI scores quality against Beacon's standards: person-centred language, specificity, outcome recording, support plan alignment.
3
Beacon polishes note for spelling, grammar and professionalism while preserving factual accuracy. Gives feedback on what to improve.
4
Support staff accepts: "This is an accurate factual depiction." Beacon never overwrites without human consent.
5
Scores hidden from staff. Focus on quality improvement, not metric chasing. Compliance team sees scores internally.

Known Limitations

"AI Would Rather Not" — AI refuses to reframe notes mentioning suicide, self-harm, or certain sensitive content. These notes are flagged for manual review by the compliance team. This is a known limitation, not a bug — human oversight is required for sensitive cases.

Live Demo
Compare Beacon against existing systems — interactive walkthrough

System Comparison

CapabilityLegacy / SpreadsheetsBeacon
Support Note EntryManual — Word docs / emailGuided forms with auto-QA
Compliance TrackingManual audit — monthlyReal-time dashboards
Risk AlertsRelies on staff memoryAutomated flagging
Support Plan ReviewsCalendar reminders (often missed)Auto-scheduled with alerts
QA ScoringSample-based — 5% of notesAI scores 100% of notes
Incident ReportingPaper forms / email chainsDigital with auto-escalation
Document StorageShared drives — no versioningPer-SU with audit trail
Staff Supervision TrackingSpreadsheet / diaryIntegrated with compliance view
ReportingManual — hours of workOne-click generated reports

Try It

Use the navigation to explore each module. Every screen in this demo is a working prototype of Beacon — the same interface your team would use day-to-day. Key areas to explore:
  • Support Notes — guided entry with AI quality scoring
  • Staff Compliance — real-time staff compliance overview
  • Incidents & Risk — automated risk escalation dashboard
  • 8-Week Guide — structured onboarding workflow
  • AI Check — live AI analysis of support notes

Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)

Access to Beacon is governed by role-based permissions. Each user is assigned a role determining what data they can view and edit. All access is logged for GDPR compliance.
RoleDashboardSU ProfilesSupport NotesIncidentsSupport PlansDocumentsStaff DataSettings
AdminFullFullFullFullFullFullFullFull
ComplianceFullViewFullFullViewFullViewNone
Team LeaderFullFullFullFullFullViewTeam OnlyNone
Support StaffOwnCaseloadCreate/Edit OwnCreate/ViewEdit Own SUsUpload Own SUsNoneNone
HousingViewViewNoneNoneNoneViewNoneNone
AccountsHB OnlyNoneNoneNoneNoneHB DocsNoneNone

GDPR Compliance Note: All user access is logged with timestamps, IP addresses, and actions performed. Data access requests (SARs) can be generated from Settings. Personal data is encrypted at rest and in transit. Data retention follows ICO guidance — inactive records are flagged for review after 12 months. Role changes require Admin approval and are audit-logged. Two-factor authentication is enforced for all Admin and Compliance roles.
